title: Sequencing library-prep front-ends (open methods only)
context: Sequencing library-preparation front-end steps (fragmentation, end-repair, adapter ligation) are essential for next-generation sequencing workflows in cancer genomics, but many commercial methods are proprietary and not openly licenseable. This unscheduled backlog task targets the subset of library-prep methods with verifiably open licenses, requires per-method license verification (this is especially critical here due to widespread commercial restrictions), and involves hazardous reagents (enzymes, solvents, spin columns with chaotropic buffers) requiring full safety documentation.
objective: Identify, license-verify, author, and ship sequencing library-prep front-end protocol records for openly-licensed methods only, each schema-valid, linted, fully provenanced, and with credentialed safety sign-off.
acceptanceCriteria: Only openly-licensed methods (CC-BY or CC0) included; license verification recorded per method with source, snapshot, SHA-256, and cited clause; no proprietary/commercial methods reproduced.
At least two library-prep front-end protocols authored, schema-valid and linter-green.
Full safety sections present (chaotropic reagents, enzyme handling, waste disposal); credentialed safety sign-off recorded.
100% of steps and safety claims carry resolvable, license-verified provenance references.
License+Ethics gate PASS per protocol; dual-use screen clear; all mandatory disclaimers present.
semver v1.0.0 + CHANGELOG per protocol; rendered Markdown/HTML produced; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
resources: lab-protocols-open gate-003 (license+ethics+dual-use gate — critical for per-method license verification)
lab-protocols-open schema-001, validate-008, lint-009, render-010 (pipeline tooling)
protocols.io (per-protocol CC-BY source candidates — license must be verified per item)
output: Committed, fully-reviewed sequencing library-prep protocol records (openly-licensed methods only) with rendered outputs, per-method license evidence, and all review sign-offs; CI green; commits DCO signed-off.
